description: Deep single-case evidence module covering Abraham Lincoln's rhetoric (1838-1865): a multi-tier annotated corpus (core, validation, reference sets), stage-by-stage annotation artifacts, and methodology documentation.
maintainerNotes: Lincoln's own writings are public domain; third-party commentary and translations require rights review before import (see docs/architecture/imported-projects.md). Corpus and draft-claim import scheduled for Milestones 15-16; no artifacts imported yet as of Milestone 14.

description: Comparative corpus evidence module covering multiple historical cases under Koenigsberg's Law of Sacrifice (American Revolution, Napoleon, Lincoln, Hitler): a multi-case stratified corpus with manifest-driven pipeline and cross-case synthesis research site.
maintainerNotes:
American Revolution and Napoleon have proposed PPW case IDs in data/corpora/sacrifice-law-comparative-41/cases.json, but the corresponding PPW case folders do not yet exist; create or approve those folders before importing their subsets. Rights vary by era; pre-1928 texts are generally public domain, Nazi-era translations and apparatus require review. Corpus import scheduled for Milestone 17; no artifacts imported yet as of Milestone 14.

Reliability sampling design (revised for 41-document corpus, PPW #192): The expanded 41-document stratified corpus requires updated inter-annotator and multi-model sampling proportions. Revised design: (1) Multi-model comparison runs — 15% sample (~6 documents), minimum 1 document per active PPW case (american-civil-war-union, nazi-germany); American Revolution and Napoleon subsets deferred until their PPW case folders are created. (2) Human inter-annotator adjudication — 20% sample (~8 documents), minimum 2 documents per active case, conducted at the evidence-review and argument-review PPW gates before claims advance to reviewed-claim status. (3) Score-review gate sampling — all claims proposed for promoted-finding undergo score-consistency review against existing PPW case scores before promotion. Sampling is proportional within each case stratum to preserve cross-case balance. Source: sacrifice-law-workbench #183.
